title: P300 dataset from Hoffmann et al 2008
description: A P300 brain-computer interface dataset comprising EEG recordings from 8 subjects (4 disabled, 4 able-bodied) performing a visual oddball task with image stimuli. The dataset includes 32-channel EEG data sampled at 2048 Hz from subjects counting target images among randomly presented non-target images, with performance evaluated using linear discriminant analysis classifiers. This derivative dataset is derived from the original Hoffmann et al. 2008 study and serves as a benchmark for BCI algorithm development and evaluation.
methods_description: EEG data were acquired using a Biosemi ActiveTwo system with 32 active electrodes at 2048 Hz sampling rate. Subjects performed a visual P300 oddball task where they silently counted target images (one of six: television, telephone, lamp, door, window, radio) presented in random sequences with 400 ms inter-stimulus intervals and 100 ms flash duration. Four sessions per subject with 6 runs each were recorded in offline mode without feedback.
